industries.name: Industrial & Scientific Lasers
uses: Yb:YAG and Yb-fiber lasers at 1030–1080nm are the fastest-growing industrial laser technology — Yb³⁺ has a simple two-level-like laser scheme with high quantum efficiency (~91%), very low heat generation, and excellent slope efficiency. Delivers kilowatt to 100kW output for sheet metal cutting, welding, and additive manufacturing

Yb-fiber lasers have largely replaced CO₂ lasers in industrial metalworking — higher beam quality (M² ~1.05), fiber delivery to robotic cutting heads, and lower operating cost make Yb-fiber the default for sheet metal fabrication globally

Yb:KGW and Yb:KYW ultrashort pulse (femtosecond and picosecond) lasers for micromachining of glass, silicon, and ceramics without thermal damage — used in display glass cutting, semiconductor packaging, and precision medical device manufacturing
Yb:glass amplifiers in petawatt laser systems for inertial confinement fusion research and high-intensity laser-matter interaction experiments at NIF (National Ignition Facility) and ELI facilities
Yb-doped fiber as gain medium in chirped pulse amplification (CPA) systems for ultrafast laser research and industrial applications

companies.name: Trumpf
companies.use: World's largest industrial laser manufacturer. Produces TruFiber, TruDisk (Yb:YAG thin-disk), and TruPulse Yb-based laser systems for sheet metal cutting, welding, and additive manufacturing. Major consumer of ytterbium-doped gain media and fiber.

companies.name: IPG Photonics
companies.use: World's largest fiber laser manufacturer. Produces Yb-doped fiber lasers from 100W to 100kW for industrial cutting, welding, and 3D printing of metals. Also supplies Yb-fiber components to other laser manufacturers globally.

companies.name: nLIGHT
companies.use: Major Yb-fiber laser manufacturer supplying kilowatt-class lasers for defense (directed energy), materials processing, and aerospace applications. Produces Yb-doped fiber and complete laser systems.

industries.name: Optical Atomic Clocks & Quantum Sensing
uses: Ytterbium optical lattice clocks: Yb atoms cooled to microkelvin temperatures and trapped in a 3D optical lattice are interrogated at 578nm (¹S₀ → ³P₀ clock transition) achieving frequency stability of ~10⁻¹⁹ — 1000× more precise than cesium microwave clocks and the most accurate clocks ever built
Yb optical clocks at NIST, PTB, and RIKEN are redefining the second — the next SI redefinition of the second will likely be based on an optical transition, possibly in Yb or Sr

Yb-based optical atomic clocks for relativistic geodesy: clock comparisons between sites at different elevations can measure gravitational potential differences at centimeter height resolution — applications in geophysics, hydrology, and volcano monitoring
Yb tweezer arrays for neutral atom quantum computing — similar to Rb and Cs, Yb atoms in optical tweezers are a leading platform for quantum computation and quantum simulation

companies.name: NIST (National Institute of Standards and Technology)
companies.use: Operates world-leading Yb optical lattice clocks achieving 10⁻¹⁹ systematic uncertainty — the most accurate clocks in the world. Yb clock research at NIST informs the future redefinition of the SI second and applications in relativistic geodesy.

companies.name: Atom Computing
companies.use: Quantum computing company developing ytterbium-171 neutral atom qubit arrays in optical tweezers — Yb's nuclear spin-1/2 and metastable clock state make it attractive for long-coherence-time quantum computation.

industries.name: Specialty Alloys & Phosphors
uses: Ytterbium addition to stainless steels and aluminum alloys as grain refiner — improves mechanical properties and surface finish in specialty alloy applications
Yb²⁺-doped phosphors for thermographic temperature sensing — Yb²⁺ emission shifts with temperature, enabling non-contact temperature measurement by luminescence spectroscopy

Ytterbium-doped upconverting nanoparticles (Yb/Er, Yb/Tm co-doped): Yb³⁺ acts as sensitizer absorbing 980nm NIR light and transferring energy to Er³⁺ or Tm³⁺ activators for upconversion bioimaging and anti-counterfeiting
Yb-doped optical fiber for distributed temperature and strain sensing using Brillouin and Raman backscattering — used in structural health monitoring of bridges, pipelines, and power cables

companies.name: Nanograde AG
companies.use: Produces ytterbium-doped upconverting nanoparticles (NaYF₄:Yb/Er, NaYF₄:Yb/Tm) for bioimaging, security printing, and photovoltaic light management applications.

companies.name: Coherent (formerly II-VI)
companies.use: Major producer of Yb-doped laser gain media including Yb:YAG crystals, Yb:KGW, and Yb-doped optical fiber for industrial and scientific laser systems. Supplies gain media to Trumpf, IPG, and other laser manufacturers.
